message:
feat(stdlib): add expand_number (FreeBSD decimal-fraction size parser) (bd-xxkxk)

FreeBSD libutil expand_number(buf, num) — parse a non-negative
human-readable size with optional decimal fraction ("1.5K" → 1536)
and one-character size suffix into a uint64. Companion to NetBSD
dehumanize_number (already shipped) but with two key differences:
(1) writes uint64_t, no negative numbers; (2) supports decimal
fractions via integer scaling.

* core::stdlib::expand_number::parse returns
  Result<u64, ExpandError> with Invalid / Overflow variants.
  Suffix multipliers k/K=1024, m/M=1024^2, g/G=1024^3, t/T=1024^4,
  p/P=1024^5, e/E=1024^6 — note: NO b/B (FreeBSD spec deliberately
  omits it, unlike NetBSD dehumanize_number). Rejects negative
  signs, fractional parts without a suffix, hex prefixes, embedded
  whitespace, trailing garbage. Decimal fraction is integer-scaled
  via u128 intermediate so very long fractional accumulators
  (frac_value ≈ 10^19) don't spuriously overflow.
* stdlib_abi::expand_number shim handles NUL-terminated input,
  NULL-pointer guards (returns -1+EINVAL), and maps Invalid →
  EINVAL / Overflow → ERANGE per FreeBSD documentation.

24 core unit tests (RFC vectors, every suffix, fraction integer
scaling, ".5K" rejected as missing-integer, "1.5" rejected as
fraction-without-suffix, very-long-fraction truncation, u64
boundary cases) + 10 abi integration tests (errno mapping, NULL
safety, every documented suffix, b/B-rejected, negative-rejected,
fraction-without-suffix-rejected). Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All
builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
